title: Log event `data` for `type`: membership.add
summary: Declares that an actor was added to a channel (or similar membership scope). v1.2 core does not require a portable crypto proof; origin enforcement is the practical baseline (spec §13.1).

channel_id.description: Channel id `relay:channel:...` as known to the origin.

member_actor.description: Actor id `relay:actor:...` added to the channel.

role.description: Optional role token (e.g. member, moderator) — string enum to be profiled; unknown values are opaque.

target_rules.description: `target` MAY be `channel_id` for routing; if present, it SHOULD be consistent with `data.channel_id`.

validation_rules: `channel_id` and `member_actor` MUST be non-empty strings in the `relay:channel:…` and `relay:actor:…` id formats the deployment uses.
Origin enforces that the event `actor` is allowed to add members per channel policy (out of band to the registry file).
Same membership represented twice: origin policy (reject duplicate, idempotency key, or supersede) — not fixed by the registry v1.

compatibility.forward: Optional keys: ignore safely for v1 readers.
compatibility.backward: Removing `channel_id` or `member_actor` is a breaking change (new file `v2`).
compatibility.version_bump: Adding new **required** keys requires `v2` or a new `type` (avoid silent split-brain names).
